title: Star Wars: The Magic of Myth
content:
{{Top|rwm|italics=1}}
{{Youmay|Star Wars: The Magic of Myth, the traveling exhibition|[[Star Wars: The Magic of Myth (book)|''Star Wars: The Magic of Myth'']], the official book of the exhibition}}
[[File:MOMfront.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The entrance to the Smithsonian Exhibition of Star Wars: the Magic of Myth]]
'''''Star Wars'': The Magic of Myth''' is a traveling exhibition created by the Smithsonian Institute, featuring props and costumes used in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' films, but focusing primarily on the mythic themes behind [[George Lucas]]' fantasy worlds and characters using [[Wikipedia:Joseph Campbell|Joseph Campbell's]] influential book [[Wikipedia:The Hero with a Thousand Faces|''The Hero With a Thousand Faces'']] as a model.

==Props==
*[[Anakin Skywalker's first lightsaber/Legends|Anakin Skywalker's first lightsaber]]
[[File:MOMdeathstar_mfalcon.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] with a painting of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] from the original Smithsonian Exhibition]]
*[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]
*[[Darth Maul's double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|Darth Maul's double-bladed lightsaber]]
*[[Luke Skywalker's green lightsaber/Legends|Luke Skywalker's green lightsaber]]
*[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]
*A [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] training [[Remote/Legends|remote]]
*[[IT-O interrogation droid/Legends|IT-O interrogation droid]]
*A [[FX-7/Legends|FX-7]] medical droid
*A [[2-1B/Legends|2-1B]] medical droid
*[[Geonosian executioner cart]]
*[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]

*[[Macrobinoculars/Legends|Macrobinoculars]]
[[File:MOMbobafett.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett's]] armor from the original Smithsonian Exhibition]]

==Artwork==
In various parts of the exhibit there conceptual art and design pieces by [[Ralph McQuarrie]], [[Doug Chiang]], [[John Mollo]], [[Joe Johnston]], [[Norman Reynolds]], [[Nilo Rodis-Jamero]], [[Paul LeBlanc]], [[David Carson]] and [[Marilee Heyer]] as well as the conceptual storyboards by [[Alex Tavoularis]].

[[File:MOMforce.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An interactive exhibit where visitor's could use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to raise the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-Wing]] from the swamp on [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]]]]

==Contents==
*[[Anthony Daniels]] was a guest at the exhibition's opening in 2002 along with [[Mary Oyaya]] and [[Nalini Krishan]] as guests in January 2003.
*The props and costumes from [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']] were only part of the exhibition when it toured Sydney.

==Tour Schedule==
*[http://www.nasm.si.edu/exhibitions/StarWars/guide.htm Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum] October 29, 1997 through January 31, 1999
*[http://www.sdmart.org The San Diego Museum of Art] September 25, 1999 through January 2, 2000
*[http://www.artsmia.org The Minneapolis Institute of Arts] February 27, 2000 through June 4, 2000
*[http://www.fieldmuseum.org The Field Museum, Chicago] July 15, 2000 through January 7, 2001
*[http://www.mfah.org The Museum of Fine Arts, Houston] March 11, 2001, through June 24, 2001
*[http://www.toledomuseum.org The Toledo Museum of Art], August 5, 2001 through January 5, 2002
*[http://www.brooklynmuseum.org The Brooklyn Museum of Art], February 16, 2002 through June 9, 2002
*[http://www.powerhousemuseum.com/previous/starwars.asp Powerhouse Museum, Sydney] 18 September 2002 through 23 February 2003
